source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $AD \perp BC$ at point $D$. Point $E$ is on $AC$, and $BE$ intersects $AD$ at point $F$. If $BF=AC$ and $DF=DC$, what is the sum of $\angle 1$ and $\angle 2$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Since $AD\perp BC$ at point $D$,\\
it follows that $\angle BDF=\angle ADC=90^\circ$,\\
In $Rt\triangle BDF$ and $Rt\triangle ADC$,\\
$\left\{\begin{array}{l}BF=AC\\ DF=DC\end{array}\right.$,\\
therefore $Rt\triangle BDF \cong Rt\triangle ADC\left(HL\right)$,\\
thus $\angle DBF=\angle 2$, $BD=AD$,\\
hence $\angle DBA=\angle DAB=45^\circ$,\\
thus $\angle 1+\angle 2=\angle 1+\angle DBF=\angle DBA=45^\circ$,\\
therefore the sum of $\angle 1$ and $\angle 2$ is $\boxed{45^\circ}$.
